2026-03-13.thesis: Strait of Hormuz closure from Iran war lifts tanker rates
2026-03-13.exit_case: Strait of Hormuz closure already reported on 2026-03-09; catalyst is public and resolved.
2026-03-13.assessment: BWET has gained from elevated tanker rates following the Strait of Hormuz closure, but the closure event is now public and likely priced in, limiting further edge.
2026-03-13.exit_advice: Exit position as the specific catalyst (closure) has already occurred and is now resolved.

2026-03-27.thesis: NexGen Rook I CNSC approval & construction license issued March 5
2026-03-27.exit_case: CNSC approval and construction license were issued March 5 — three weeks before entry. Catalyst is fully resolved and public.
2026-03-27.assessment: The discrete catalyst — CNSC approval of Rook I environmental assessment and issuance of the Licence to Prepare Site and Construction — was granted March 5, 2026, before this position was even opened (March 27). Insider Monkey's March 24 article confirms and frames it as a 'turning point for its uranium buildout.' The specific one-time government approval is done, public, and being discussed in mainstream financial media. There is no residual pending decision or binary event to wait for. The only remaining value would be general uranium theme upside, which is not what this event-driven book is for.
2026-03-27.exit_advice: Close the position immediately. The specific catalyst (CNSC Rook I approval + construction license) was issued March 5 and is 22 days past by entry date. The edge was pricing a discrete approval event — that event is public and priced. Holding on after this is thematic conviction, not event-driven alpha. Exit now and recycle capital.

2026-04-03.thesis: Trump adjusts aluminum tariffs on Liberation Day anniversary
2026-04-03.exit_case: Trump already overhauled aluminum duties on April 2; AA already jumped 22%. Catalyst fully resolved and priced same-day as entry.
2026-04-03.assessment: AA gapped +22% on the day the tariff overhaul was announced (Apr 2). The catalyst that was the premise for this trade — Trump adjusting aluminum duties on the Liberation Day anniversary — has now occurred. The move is in the price. This is a textbook example of entering on the same day the catalyst fires, giving zero pre-event edge.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: Sell immediately. The aluminum tariff adjustment has been publicly announced and AA has already re-rated +22%. No asymmetric edge remains. Holding here is chasing, not harvesting a catalyst.

2026-05-08.thesis: Oklo NRC reactor design approval decision on May 12
2026-05-08.assessment: Oklo stock surged 46% in April with high anticipation for NRC reactor design approval decision on May 12. Catalyst is pending; no resolution yet.
2026-05-08.exit_advice: Hold until NRC decision on May 12; re-evaluate thesis based on outcome and post-decision price action.

2026-05-15.thesis: Oklo NRC reactor design approval decision on May 12
2026-05-15.exit_case: May 12 decision date passed three days ago with zero reported NRC action — the catalytic event window is closed.
2026-05-15.assessment: The May 12 NRC decision date has passed (now May 15). The post-catalyst news cycle contains zero mention of an NRC ruling — neither approval grant nor denial. JPMorgan's May 15 note discusses SMR outlook without referencing any Oklo-specific NRC action, and the insidermonkey piece is a generic listicle. Either the decision was delayed (breaking the date-specific entry premise) or the outcome was non-event. The time-sensitive binary edge from the May 12 date has degraded entirely.
2026-05-15.exit_advice: The specific May 12 NRC decision date has passed with no reported outcome. Whether delayed or non-announced, the binary catalyst window you entered on is closed. Sell and close the event.

2026-05-29.assessment: Event initiated on announcement of advanced DOE talks, which occurred May 27 and has been public for a week. No further specific catalyst pending.
